IERC20","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Supersedes v2 (which used a raw, frontrunnable {acceptJob}). The {Status} enum and {Job}         struct are byte-identical to v2: a commitment is a pending *bid*, not a job state, so the         job stays Funded until a reveal lands and every downstream reader/indexer is unchanged.         Commitments live in a side mapping. Lifecycle:         createJob -> fund -> commitAccept -> revealAccept -> submitWork -> complete | reject | claimRefund.         Funds safety uses checks-effects-interactions: status is updated before any token transfer.         commit-reveal defeats the *hash-copy* frontrun completely; a narrow residual (a bot that         speculatively pre-commits to the same job could still race the reveal) is mitigated by         routing the reveal tx through a private mempool \u2014 defense-in-depth, see docs/MEV.md.         None of these are Cobo Agentic Wallet methods \u2014 they are our own settlement primitives.","events":{"AcceptCommitted(bytes32,uint64)":{"details":"Commit phase: intentionally JOB-AGNOSTIC. Emitting the jobId here would re-create the      exact frontrunning surface commit-reveal exists to close, so we emit only the opaque hash."},"JobAccepted(uint256,address)":{"details":"Reveal phase: same name + shape as v2's JobAccepted, so dashboards/indexers are unchanged."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"claimRefund(uint256)":{"details":"Allowed from Funded (nobody revealed), Accepted (provider never submitted), or         Submitted (evaluator never decided). Outstanding commitments are inert \u2014 they revert         on reveal once the job leaves Funded \u2014 so they never block this backstop."},"commitAccept(bytes32)":{"details":"Job-agnostic and permissionless by design \u2014 even the client/evaluator may commit;         they simply can never {revealAccept} (checked there). Commit touches NO job state, so         it cannot reserve, block, or DoS a job.","params":{"commitment":"MUST equal keccak256(abi.encode(jobId, msg.sender, salt)) where `salt` is         a 32-byte secret the caller keeps until reveal. The hash leaks neither the jobId nor         anything reusable: bound to msg.sender, a copied commitment is worthless to others."}},"createJob(address,uint256,bytes32,uint64)":{"details":"Does NOT move funds - call {fund} after approving USDC to this contract."},"fund(uint256)":{"details":"Funding BEFORE acceptance means the board shows genuinely-funded open jobs that         providers can trust before committing work."},"revealAccept(uint256,bytes32)":{"params":{"jobId":"the job being claimed (hidden during commit, disclosed only now).","salt":"the secret used to build the commitment."}},"submitWork(uint256,bytes32,string)":{"params":{"deliverableHash":"keccak256 of the deliverable content (integrity commitment).","irysId":"Irys data-item id; fetch at the gateway and verify keccak256 == deliverableHash."}}},"title":"AgentWorksEscrowV3","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"claimRefund(uint256)":{"notice":"Client reclaims escrowed funds after the deadline if the job was never settled."},"commitAccept(bytes32)":{"notice":"Step 1 of the sealed accept race: publish a hidden bid."},"commitInfo(bytes32)":{"notice":"Inspect a sealed bid (for off-chain UIs computing the reveal window). Returns a zero         commitBlock for an unknown commitment."},"complete(uint256)":{"notice":"Evaluator accepts the deliverable; pays the provider."},"createJob(address,uint256,bytes32,uint64)":{"notice":"Client posts an OPEN job (no provider named) - price, evaluator, spec hash, deadline."},"fund(uint256)":{"notice":"Client escrows the job amount in USDC (requires prior ERC-20 approval)."},"getJob(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns the full job record; reverts if the job does not exist."},"nextJobId()":{"notice":"Monotonic job id counter (first job is id 1)."},"reject(uint256)":{"notice":"Evaluator rejects the deliverable; refunds the client."},"revealAccept(uint256,bytes32)":{"notice":"Step 2 of the sealed accept race: open the bid and claim the job. The first valid         reveal wins (Funded -> Accepted); any later reveal reverts because the job left Funded."},"revealDelayBlocks()":{"notice":"Minimum blocks between a commit and its reveal. Forces the commitment to be buried         under at least one block before it can be opened (commit + reveal cannot share a         block, which would re-expose the plaintext race). Immutable, set at deploy."},"revealWindowBlocks()":{"notice":"Maximum additional blocks (after the delay) a commitment stays revealable. Past this         the commitment expires, so a stale-but-valid commitment cannot be hoarded to snipe a         job much later. Immutable, set at deploy."},"submitWork(uint256,bytes32,string)":{"notice":"Provider submits the deliverable's content hash + the Irys id where it's stored."},"token()":{"notice":"The settlement token (USDC, or MockUSDC for tests). Immutable."}},"notice":"Open-marketplace settlement layer between distrustful agents, hardened against         frontrunning of the accept race. A Client posts and funds a job WITHOUT naming a         provider; ANY agent may then claim the funded job \u2014 but instead of a single plaintext         {acceptJob} that leaks the jobId to the public mempool (v2), claiming is a sealed         two-phase COMMIT-REVEAL:           1. {commitAccept}(commitment)  \u2014 publishes only an opaque hash; reveals neither the              targeted jobId nor anything a frontrunner can reuse.           2. {revealAccept}(jobId, salt) \u2014 opens the commitment after a short block delay; the              first valid reveal wins the job (Funded -> Accepted). A copied commitment is useless              to anyone else because it is bound to the committer's address.         The claimer then submits a deliverable content-hash; the Evaluator accepts (pay Provider)         or rejects (refund Client). An unresolved funded job is reclaimable by the Client after         the deadline \u2014 including jobs nobody ever revealed.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/AgentWorksEscrowV3.sol":"AgentWorksEscrowV3"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/"]},"sources":{"src/AgentWorksEscrowV3.sol":{"keccak256":"0x18e68c03fb4881b31f05baf564744a68964eeaa8f1be2526b83d04725eda8cb7","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://5d0cea06bfbb77114af51b743850d0ddc47bdf9ba91a833daa69158363209387","dweb:/ipfs/QmZdHkCaN8pHVuwfYzaWZrQMYxLYKLv2HEwwediy5y7hyS"]}},"version":1}

claimRefund(uint256).details: Allowed from Funded (nobody revealed), Accepted (provider never submitted), or         Submitted (evaluator never decided). Outstanding commitments are inert — they revert         on reveal once the job leaves Funded — so they never block this backstop.

commitAccept(bytes32).details: Job-agnostic and permissionless by design — even the client/evaluator may commit;         they simply can never {revealAccept} (checked there). Commit touches NO job state, so         it cannot reserve, block, or DoS a job.

params.commitment: MUST equal keccak256(abi.encode(jobId, msg.sender, salt)) where `salt` is         a 32-byte secret the caller keeps until reveal. The hash leaks neither the jobId nor         anything reusable: bound to msg.sender, a copied commitment is worthless to others.

createJob(address,uint256,bytes32,uint64.details: Does NOT move funds - call {fund} after approving USDC to this contract.

fund(uint256).details: Funding BEFORE acceptance means the board shows genuinely-funded open jobs that         providers can trust before committing work.

params.jobId: the job being claimed (hidden during commit, disclosed only now).
params.salt: the secret used to build the commitment.

params.deliverableHash: keccak256 of the deliverable content (integrity commitment).
params.irysId: Irys data-item id; fetch at the gateway and verify keccak256 == deliverableHash.

claimRefund(uint256).notice: Client reclaims escrowed funds after the deadline if the job was never settled.

commitAccept(bytes32).notice: Step 1 of the sealed accept race: publish a hidden bid.

commitInfo(bytes32).notice: Inspect a sealed bid (for off-chain UIs computing the reveal window). Returns a zero         commitBlock for an unknown commitment.

complete(uint256).notice: Evaluator accepts the deliverable; pays the provider.

createJob(address,uint256,bytes32,uint64.notice: Client posts an OPEN job (no provider named) - price, evaluator, spec hash, deadline.

fund(uint256).notice: Client escrows the job amount in USDC (requires prior ERC-20 approval).

getJob(uint256).notice: Returns the full job record; reverts if the job does not exist.

nextJobId().notice: Monotonic job id counter (first job is id 1).

reject(uint256).notice: Evaluator rejects the deliverable; refunds the client.

revealAccept(uint256,bytes32).notice: Step 2 of the sealed accept race: open the bid and claim the job. The first valid         reveal wins (Funded -> Accepted); any later reveal reverts because the job left Funded.

revealDelayBlocks().notice: Minimum blocks between a commit and its reveal. Forces the commitment to be buried         under at least one block before it can be opened (commit + reveal cannot share a         block, which would re-expose the plaintext race). Immutable, set at deploy.

revealWindowBlocks().notice: Maximum additional blocks (after the delay) a commitment stays revealable. Past this         the commitment expires, so a stale-but-valid commitment cannot be hoarded to snipe a         job much later. Immutable, set at deploy.

submitWork(uint256,bytes32,string).notice: Provider submits the deliverable's content hash + the Irys id where it's stored.

token().notice: The settlement token (USDC, or MockUSDC for tests). Immutable.
